TSLA: Hyundai Motor Group to appoint former head of Tesla's humanoid robot program as adviser. Hyundai Motor Group announced its plan to appoint Milan Kovac, former head of Tesla's humanoid robot program, as an adviser to boost its capabilities in robotics and artificial intelligence. Kovac, who led Tesla's Optimus program and will be nominated as an outside director for Boston Dynamics, will help strategize commercialization across Hyundai's robotics platforms. This move comes as Hyundai unveiled a production version of its Atlas humanoid robot and aims to manufacture 30,000 robot units annually by 2028, seeing humanoid robots as a significant segment of the physical AI market.

Mag 7 Fatigue? MAGS Dips As Tesla, Meta And Nvidia Slip In Early 2026 - Roundhill Magnificent Seven ETF. The Roundhill Magnificent Seven ETF (MAGS) is experiencing a dip in early 2026, with Tesla, Meta, and Nvidia among its constituents slipping. This indicates a potential "Mag 7 fatigue" among investors, as the once-dominant tech mega-caps are underperforming after driving market gains for the past three years. Despite the short-term weakness, some strategists view this as a temporary pause, expecting AI-related names within the group to continue driving S&P 500 earnings growth.

Tesla agrees to mediation to settle racial harassment lawsuit. Tesla has agreed to private mediation to resolve a 2023 lawsuit filed by the U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC) alleging widespread racial harassment at its Fremont, California manufacturing plant. The lawsuit claims Tesla tolerated racial slurs, stereotyping, and hostile graffiti, and retaliated against employees who opposed the harassment. Mediation talks are expected to occur in March or April.

Tesla's Ending Sales of Self-Driving Software Might Be a Move to Make Musk the First Trillionaire. Tesla is discontinuing outright sales of its Full Self-Driving (FSD) software, transitioning to a subscription-only model of $99 per month starting February 14. This shift has sparked speculation that it's a strategic move to help Elon Musk achieve a compensation goal tied to reaching 10 million active FSD subscriptions, a prerequisite for him potentially becoming the world's first trillionaire. Critics also suggest the change allows Tesla to manage expectations better, given the persistent technical limitations of the Level 2 FSD system.
TGT: Could a Short Squeeze Start Brewing in BARK Stock Following a New Go-Private Offer?. Bark (BARK) shares surged over 30% after a non-binding $0.90 per-share go-private offer from an insider group led by CEO Matt Meeker, presenting a significant premium to recent prices. With more than 10% of the float sold short, this offer could trigger a short squeeze, intensifying volatility. Despite recent financial struggles and lower guidance, analysts see potential upside, with a "Moderate Buy" consensus and an average price target of $2.33, implying a 195% potential increase.

More than 100 clergy members stage sit-in at Target headquarters, demanding action in response to 'ICE surge'. Over 100 clergy members from Minnesota staged a sit-in at Target's corporate headquarters in downtown Minneapolis, demanding that the company publicly call for an end to ICE's "surge" in the state and affirm Target stores as "4th Amendment workplaces." The action followed a press conference where they urged Target to "stand up for their workers" and community, citing the company's silence after ICE agents reportedly detained U.S. citizen employees inside a Target store. The clergy leaders, who also delivered a letter signed by thousands of faith leaders, remained in the lobby for hours seeking a meeting with CEO Brian Cornell.

Target will now sell meat from ButcherBox. Target is expanding its wellness-oriented food offerings by introducing fresh, grass-fed, grass-finished beef products from ButcherBox in nearly 1,500 stores nationwide. This marks ButcherBox's first major foray beyond its subscription-based direct-to-consumer model into brick-and-mortar retail, aiming to reach a broader customer base and align with increasing consumer demand for health-conscious meat options. The move follows ButcherBox's previous collaboration with Target through its online marketplace.

Costco stock inches up after insider sale notice as Bernstein sticks with $1,146 target. Costco Wholesale Corp (COST) shares rose slightly after a Form 144 filing indicated an insider's intention to sell stock, despite which Bernstein maintained its "Outperform" rating and $1,146 price target, calling the stock "undervalued." The market is sensitive to any signals affecting consumer demand or retail margins, with insider sale notices often prompting overreactions. Costco also reported strong December net sales and comparable sales growth, with its annual shareholder meeting taking place online.
SBUX: Citigroup Raises Price Target for Starbucks (SBUX) to $94 | SBUX Stock News. Citigroup analyst Jon Tower has maintained a 'Neutral' rating for Starbucks (SBUX) but raised the price target to $94 from $83, marking a 13.25% increase. This update comes amidst varied analyst opinions, with other firms like Mizuho and Barclays also adjusting their price targets, and TD Cowen and BTIG reiterating their respective ratings. Starbucks, a global coffee brand with nearly 41,000 cafes, receives an average analyst target price of $94.81 and a "Outperform" consensus recommendation.
DIS: Disney streamlines marketing into one unit focused on cohesion, agility. The Walt Disney Company is consolidating its marketing operations into a single, enterprise-wide organization led by Asad Ayaz, who will serve as chief marketing and brand officer. This restructuring aims to enhance brand consistency, agility, and innovation across Disney Entertainment, Disney Experiences, and ESPN divisions. Ayaz, previously chief brand officer and president of marketing for The Walt Disney Studios, will report directly to CEO Bob Iger.

Ding Xuexiang Meets with CEO of The Walt Disney Company of the United States Robert Alan Iger. Ding Xuexiang, Vice Premier of China, met with Disney CEO Robert Alan Iger in Beijing. Ding emphasized China's commitment to high-quality development, opening up, and optimizing its business environment, welcoming Disney and other foreign enterprises to invest and participate in Chinese modernization. Iger expressed confidence in China's development and a commitment to expanding Disney's investment and promoting bilateral exchanges.

The Walt Disney Company Earns 10 Actor Award® Nominations. The Walt Disney Company has received 10 Actor Award® nominations across its various content brands and studios. The awards ceremony will take place on March 1, and Disney Legend Harrison Ford will be honored with the SAG-AFTRA Life Achievement Award. Notable nominations include "The Beast in Me" for acting performances, "Only Murders in the Building" and "The Bear" for ensemble comedy, and "Andor" for stunt performance.

The Walt Disney Studios Announces Lucasfilm Leadership Transition. Kathleen Kennedy is stepping down as Lucasfilm President after nearly 14 years to return to full-time producing, focusing on upcoming films like The Mandalorian and Grogu and Star Wars: Starfighter. Dave Filoni will now lead Lucasfilm as President and Chief Creative Officer, alongside Lynwen Brennan as Co-President. Both Filoni and Brennan, long-time executives at Lucasfilm, will report to Disney Entertainment Co-Chairman Alan Bergman, guiding the studio into its next chapter of storytelling.

Disney at CES 2026: Building Worlds Big and Small. At CES 2026, Disney showcased its innovation in immersive storytelling, highlighting collaborations with LEGO for interactive play and Haddy for large-scale 3D printing. The company introduced LEGO Smart Play, which integrates Star Wars storytelling with physical and digital play, and demonstrated Haddy's MicroFactory in producing a 3D-printed canoe for Disneyland's Jungle Cruise. These initiatives exemplify Disney's strategy of uniting technology and creativity to build engaging experiences across various scales, from miniature toys to theme park attractions.

CORREÇÃO-Netflix fecha acordo global para transmitir filmes da Sony Pictures após exibição em cinemas Por Reuters. Netflix and Sony Pictures Entertainment have reached a global multi-year agreement for Netflix to stream Sony Pictures films, including "Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse", after their theatrical release. Netflix will be the exclusive streaming destination for these films for the first 18 months, after which they will move to Disney. This deal ensures a steady supply of licensed content for Netflix and provides Sony with a distribution channel for its cinematic releases.

Leslie Iwerks Discusses Her New Film ‘Disneyland Handcrafted’. Filmmaker Leslie Iwerks discusses her new documentary, "Disneyland Handcrafted," which offers a rare look at the challenges faced during the creation of Disneyland, using previously unseen archival footage and audio. Iwerks, whose family has deep roots in Disney history, explains how the film highlights Walt Disney's visionary approach to media and the groundbreaking innovations behind the park. The documentary will premiere on Disney+ and Disney YouTube on January 22.
NKE: Loop Industries targets Q4 2027 completion for India plant amid Nike anchor deal and expanding textile-to-textile demand. Loop Industries is targeting the completion of its textile-to-textile recycling plant in India by the fourth quarter of 2027. This ambitious project is bolstered by an anchor deal with Nike, highlighting the increasing demand for sustainable textile solutions. The plant aims to address the significant challenge of textile waste by converting discarded materials into virgin-quality polyester, supporting a circular economy.
